CAT05-036.headline: ADHD and Work
CAT05-036.subtitle: Challenges and accommodations
CAT05-036.figureLine: A single seated clay figure leans forward with both hands steadying its workspace, posture engaged and capable, conveying focus found once the conditions finally fit.
CAT05-036.sceneLine: A cluster of scattered soft light amber fragments gently rearranges into a clear, supportive amber accent framework around the figure, suggesting an environment reshaped to let a different mind do its best work.

CAT08-043.headline: The Marshmallow Test
CAT08-043.subtitle: What it actually proved about delayed gratification
CAT08-043.figureLine: A single standing clay figure pauses with one hand resting calmly near a small held form, posture patient and unhurried, conveying a quiet choice to wait.
CAT08-043.sceneLine: A single soft light amber rounded form rests beside the figure while a second, brighter amber accent shape glows softly in the near distance, suggesting the gentle pull between now and later.

CAT13-041.headline: ADHD in Adults
CAT13-041.subtitle: Symptoms, diagnosis, and treatment
CAT13-041.figureLine: A single standing clay figure gathers several drifting elements toward itself with open hands, posture attentive and self-aware, conveying long-overlooked patterns finally coming into focus.
CAT13-041.sceneLine: Loose amber accent threads that once wandered freely draw together into one legible, steady pale amber line beside the figure, suggesting scattered experience finally understood as a whole.

CAT13-042.headline: ADHD in Women and Girls
CAT13-042.subtitle: Underdiagnosis and unique presentation
CAT13-042.figureLine: A single seated clay figure turns gently toward a quiet form long held in shadow, posture recognizing and tender, conveying something true at last being seen.
CAT13-042.sceneLine: A faint, easily missed pale amber outline beside the figure slowly fills with warm soft light amber glow, suggesting a presentation long overlooked finally coming clearly into view.

CAT13-043.figureLine: A single standing clay figure rests in a calm, settled stance with weight evenly grounded, posture self-possessed and at ease, conveying a mind comfortably its own.
CAT13-043.sceneLine: A precise, intricate amber accent pattern arranges itself in clean repeating order around the figure, rendered in soft light amber, suggesting a distinct and valid way of perceiving the world.

CAT13-044.subtitle: Relief, grief, and identity shift
CAT13-044.figureLine: A single seated clay figure holds one hand to its center while the other reaches softly outward, posture caught between settling and reaching, conveying both relief and quiet grief at once.
CAT13-044.sceneLine: Behind the figure a faded pale amber reflection lingers while a clearer amber accent form gathers ahead, the two held in soft light amber balance, suggesting a self being gently re-understood.

CAT13-045.headline: ADHD and Autism Overlap
CAT13-045.subtitle: Understanding where the two intersect

CAT13-045.sceneLine: Two distinct amber accent currents flow toward the figure and merge into a single warm soft light amber band, suggesting two neurotypes meeting and overlapping in one person.

CAT13-046.subtitle: How the diagnosis differs across systems
CAT13-046.figureLine: A single standing clay figure stands steady while the same form is softly echoed twice beside it, posture unchanged across each, conveying one experience named in different ways.
CAT13-046.sceneLine: Two slightly different pale amber frames overlay the same central amber accent shape near the figure, suggesting one reality described through two different diagnostic lenses.

CAT13-047.headline: Executive Dysfunction
CAT13-047.subtitle: ADHD beyond attention
CAT13-047.figureLine: A single seated clay figure reaches toward several small forms just beyond easy grasp, posture willing and effortful, conveying intention that meets a real gap in follow-through.
CAT13-047.sceneLine: A row of soft light amber stepping forms beside the figure has one section gently set apart, the amber accent path bridging carefully across the gap, suggesting the hidden work of starting and finishing.

CAT13-048.headline: Autism Masking
CAT13-048.subtitle: Why hiding autistic traits leads to burnout
CAT13-048.figureLine: A single seated clay figure lets a smooth outer shell ease away from its shoulders, posture finally unguarded and exhaling, conveying the relief of no longer holding a performance.
CAT13-048.sceneLine: A polished pale amber surface beside the figure softly parts to reveal a warmer, truer soft light amber form beneath, suggesting the rest that comes when a hidden self can stop concealing.

CAT13-050.subtitle: Support at work and school
